results.title: Android 17 Beta 1 Problems solution was like enjoying Rising Star Game – The Chilling Stories
results.content: No comments on Android 17 Beta 1 Problems solution was like enjoying Rising Star Game. # Android 17 Beta 1 Problems solution was like enjoying Rising Star Game. > It was when the notification appeared — *“Android 17 Beta 1 is available”* — it felt like unlocking a new secret level. > For the first hour, everything felt new and exciting. I researched about this in forums and found many users frustrated with these problems. I thought what would I do if I experienced the same kind of issues. > Just like the strategy-based simulation game **Rising Star**, Android 17 Beta 1 isn’t broken — it’s a new level that demands smarter strategy. If you are not a tech user, Before solving problems, you must understand what a beta actually is. So, what happens is that this alone increases battery drain and background CPU activity. The second step I think which will be helpful to you will be to Tap the app → Restrict background activity.

results.title: How's android 17 experience so far : r/pixel_phones - Reddit
results.content: Honestly, it's not bad for a first beta; it's quite stable. Battery life is good. No major bugs. The transition between cameras seems improved.

results.title: Android Developers
results.content: ### Feedback and issues. Use the issue tracker to create new issues and to view, track, and vote for issues that you and other developers have created. ### Create and vote for issues. Before creating your own issue, check the release notes and search the lists of top open issues and recently created issues to see if someone else has already reported it. You can subscribe and vote for an issue by **starring it**. If your feedback is closed and you're still experiencing the issue on the latest build, please check the release notes and the lists of top open issues and recently created issues and **star**  the issue if it already exists. To help Google manage feedback volumes and control for duplicates, new issues submitted through the Android Beta Feedback app remain separate from those reported through the issue tracker until reviewed and verified. If not, please submit your feedback again from the Android Beta Feedback app running on the latest release.

results.title: Android 17 Beta 4.1 finally fixes the Pixel problems folks have been complaining about
results.content: Timi is the news and deals reporter for *Android Police,* who has been reporting on technology since 2008. He has worked in tech retail and also the IT space, providing hardware and software support, which gives him a unique perspective on the tech that he covers. Before joining *Android Police*, he was a news writer for *XDA*, where he eventually transitioned to covering deals. He also worked as an editor and reporter for *Neowin*, where he covered news and attended major tech events like CES. If you've got your Pixel on the beta track, you'll be happy to know that another update just dropped, bringing it to Android 17 Beta 4.1. Well, there are a lot of interesting ones, but we'll list them below verbatim from the Android Developer website so you can pick out the things that matter to you most. Personally, the one that stands out is the update that will fix the status bar issue, where it wasn't showing proper signal coverage.
